channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Pakistan evaluates spot LNG purchases to manage supply disruptions amid Iran conflict
item.description: Pakistan is exploring the option of buying liquefied natural gas (LNG) from the spot market to counter disruptions in supply triggered by the ongoing conflict in Iran. Petroleum Minister Ali Pervaiz M...

item.title: Citizen Group KOHmmunity Supports Obafemi Hamzat’s Governorship Ambition in Lagos
item.description: As Lagos prepares for the 2027 governorship election, a citizen-led group known as KOHmmunity has publicly thrown their support behind Deputy Governor Obafemi Hamzat. This group, which has presence in...

item.title: Police search for suspects after attempted arson at Finchley synagogue
item.description: Police in north London are conducting a manhunt following an attempted arson attack at Finchley Reform Synagogue. The incident occurred around 01:00 BST when two suspects, wearing balaclavas, threw a ...

item.title: Five Individuals Arrested in Laguna for Possessing Unregistered Alcohol
item.description: In Biñan, Laguna, five individuals were apprehended after authorities conducted a raid on a warehouse in Barangay San Antonio, seizing unregistered alcohol products valued at P1.5 million. The arreste...

item.title: Pakistan Plans Spot LNG Purchases Amid Iran Conflict, Strengthens Domestic Oil and Gas Output
item.description: Pakistan is facing energy supply challenges due to the ongoing war in Iran, which has disrupted regional oil and gas flows. Petroleum Minister Ali Pervaiz Malik informed that the government is looking...

item.title: Professor Kila Criticizes INEC Over Handling of ADC Leadership Dispute
item.description: Professor Anthony Kila, a respected academic in Strategy and Development, has raised concerns over the Independent National Electoral Commission’s (INEC) decision to delist the leadership of the Afric...

item.title: Guide to the best toys and gifts for six-year-olds in 2026
item.description: The Independent presents a detailed selection of the best gifts and toys for six-year-olds in 2026, based on testing with two young children and adult reviewers. The article covers a wide range of toy...

item.title: eThekwini implements corrective measures after EPWP funding suspended due to irregularities
item.description: The eThekwini Metropolitan Municipality has responded to the decision by Public Works and Infrastructure Minister Dean Macpherson to withhold Expanded Public Works Programme (EPWP) funding for the 202...

item.title: Snapchat Lays Off 1,000 Employees, Cites AI Efficiency for Restructuring
item.description: Snap Inc., the parent company of Snapchat, announced that it is laying off approximately 1,000 employees, representing about 16% of its workforce, while simultaneously eliminating 300 open positions. ...

item.title: GPS Jamming and Spoofing from Regional Conflict Disrupts Delivery Operations in Gulf Nations
item.description: In the ongoing conflict involving the US, Israel, and Iran, military forces in the Persian Gulf region have started using electronic warfare techniques like GPS jamming and spoofing to counter drones ...

item.title: Jodi Sta. Maria shares challenges and experiences while filming ‘Midnight Girls’
item.description: In the latest ABS-CBN feature, actress Jodi Sta. Maria opens up about her experience working on the film 'Midnight Girls'. She discussed how the role pushed her acting skills to new limits, requiring ...

item.title: Two Royal Canadian Navy Members Charged After Halifax Harbour Accident Resulting in Sailor's Death
item.description: The Canadian Forces Military Police have laid charges against two Royal Canadian Navy members following the death of Petty Officer 2nd Class Gregory Applin in January 2025. Applin, 38, from Shoal Cove...

item.title: Snap Inc. to reduce workforce by 16% to focus on long-term profitability
item.description: Snap Inc., the parent company of Snapchat, has announced it will be reducing its full-time workforce by around 16%, which amounts to roughly 1,000 employees. The company is also closing approximately ...

item.title: BI sees steady international travel despite Middle East conflict
item.description: The Bureau of Immigration (BI) expressed optimism on Wednesday that international passenger traffic will remain strong in the coming weeks despite ongoing tensions in the Middle East. BI Commissioner ...

item.title: South Africa’s Wastewater System Faces Critical Failures Despite Skilled Workforce
item.description: South Africa is facing a serious crisis in its wastewater treatment sector, with nearly half of its 848 plants evaluated in critical condition according to the latest Green Drop report. Only about 25%...

item.title: Government Defence Investment Plan Remains Unpublished Amid Funding Disputes
item.description: Sir Keir Starmer has not provided a date for the publication of the government's long-delayed 10-year defence investment plan, prompting criticism amid increasing concerns over UK defence readiness. T...

item.title: Application for Filipino nurses and careworkers to Japan extended until April 24, 2026
item.description: The Department of Migrant Workers (DMW) of the Philippines, in partnership with the Japan International Corporation of Welfare Services (JICWELS), has announced the extension of the deadline for appli...

item.title: Alberta farmer reports health and environmental concerns from aging oil wells on her property
item.description: Teresa Patry, a farmer and rancher from Vermilion, Alberta, is dealing with two active oil wells on her land that, according to an independent air quality assessment, emit methane and other volatile o...

item.title: Bank of Tanzania Trains Zanzibar Lawmakers on 2025 Microfinance Regulations
item.description: The Bank of Tanzania (BoT) recently organised a focused training seminar for members of the Rules and Subsidiary Legislation Committee of the Zanzibar House of Representatives. The training, held at B...

item.title: Dalawang lalaki napatay sa ambush sa Datu Paglas, Maguindanao del Sur
item.description: Dalawang lalaki ang napatay sa isang ambush sa Barangay Damawato, Datu Paglas, Maguindanao del Sur, noong Martes. Isa sa mga biktima ay nakilala bilang si Ibrahim Pigkaulan, na napatay na agad sa luga...
